note: No human-messages.json sessions exist for this date range. Era 1 was entirely agent-driven (Kai/OpenClaw). This chunk is populated from commit metadata and deep-era archaeological analysis.
author: Pastorsimon1798
narrative_arc: An AI agent named Kai scaffolds the entire project in one evening — 29 commits implementing everything from PromiseDetector to RalphLoop
key_events: Kai agent runs 29 task-jobs building full architecture
First LLM integration: 'Fix Atelier LLM integration - WORKING' (Mar 1)
deep_analysis_summary: Era 1 is the origin story. Between 21:36 on February 28 and 02:18 on March 1, a single AI agent named Kai, operating under the OpenClaw framework, produced 43 commits that scaffolded the entire Atelier project. Every commit was agent-generated. The human (Simon) directed the work from two interfaces: structured task-job dispatch for the first 27 commits, and mobile phone chat for the final phase. The PRD was ambitious but the execution revealed a template engine dressed as a creative agent: P5Generator matched keywords and returned hardcoded templates, CreativeEvaluator scored via regex, the RalphLoop passed context forward but generators ignored it. The 'two-hour gap' between last task-job dispatch and mobile-chat-directed LLM commits was not a human takeover but an interface switch. The benchmark report revealed the truth: three different prompts produced identical outputs. The system was technically complete but substantively empty.
decisions_made: Jest over Vitest (would prove poor fit for ESM TypeScript)
Static class methods as universal pattern (namespace-as-OOP)
Template-based code generation over LLM generation
decisions_rejected: LLM integration during initial build (added 2 hours later via mobile chat)
Lenia-style continuous CA (implemented Conway's Game of Life instead)
Genetic algorithms (PRD listed as Phase 1, never attempted)
Preview server rendering (built but never called from loop)
critical_finding: The entire era was agent-driven from PRD to working code. No code was written by human hands at a keyboard. The human directed from a phone. The machine built.
